sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal, prop. to stand upright; see Pilel, Hiphil, and the noun כֵּן. A secondary root is the Arab. and Eth. كَانَ, ኮነ፡ to exist, to be. As to וַיְכוּנֶּנּוּ Job 31:15 see Analyt. Ind.

Pilel כֹּונֵן.-

(1) to set up, to erect, prop. to set upright, as a throne, Psa 9:8 2Sa 7:13 hence, to confirm, to establish, to maintain, Psa 7:10 40:3 48:9 68:10 90:17 99:4 .

(2) to found, as a city, Psa 107:36 Hab 2:12 the earth, Psa 24:2 119:90 heaven, Pro 3:19.

(3) to direct, as arrows, Psa 7:13 11:2 also without the accus. חִצִּים absol. (zielen), followed by עַל of the mark, Psa 21:12. Metaph. with the omission of לֵב (for the full form, see Hiphil, No. 4), to turn one’s mind to any thing, to have in one’s mind, Job 8:8 Isa 51:13.

(4) to create, to form, used of God with regard to man, Deu 32:6 Psa 119:73 the moon and stars, Psa 8:4.

Pass. כֹּונַן.-

(1) to be established (used of one’s steps), Psa 37:23.

(2) Pass. of act. No. 3, to be prepared, Eze 28:13.

Hiphil הֵכִין i.q. Pilel.-

(1) to set up, to erect, e.g. a seat, Job 29:7 Psa 103:19 hence, to establish, Psa 89:5 2Sa 7:12 to strengthen, Psa 10:17 89:5 .

(2) to constitute, to appoint any one, e.g. a king; followed by לְ 2Sa 5:12 Jos 4:4.

(3) to found, as a sanctuary, 1Ki 6:19 an altar, Ezr 3:3 the world, the mountains, Psa 65:7 Jer 10:12 51:15 .

(4) to direct, to aim, as a weapon, followed by לְ of pers. (against any one), Psa 7:14 to set the face, Eze 4:3, one’s way, 2Ch 27:6. Specially-(a) הֵכִין לֵב לְ to apply one’s mind to do something, i.e. to purpose seriously, to take in hand, 2Ch 12:14 30:19 Ezr 7:10 and without לֵב 1Ch 28:2, הֲכִינֹותִי לִבְנֹות “I have purposed to build;” Jud. 12:6 (b) (לֵב) הֵכִין to apply the mind, 1Sa 23:22 followed by לְ of pers. (for to care for) 2Ch 29:36.-(c) הֵכִין לֵב אֶל־יְהֹוָה to direct the heart to the Lord, 1Sa 7:3 2Ch 20:33 and without אֶל־יְהֹוָה Job 11:13.

(5) to prepare, make ready, as food, Gen 43:16 deceit, Job 15:35 compare Job 27:17 39:5 , etc.-As to the Inf. absol. אָכֵן (for הָכֵן), and its use as an adverb, see that word.

Hophal, pass. of Hiphil, No. 1, Isa 16:5 of No. 2, Isa 30:33 of No. 5, Nah 2:6 Pro 21:31.

Niphal, pass. of Pilel and Hiphil.-

(1) to be set up, to rise up, Isa 2:2 Eze 16:7 (of breasts becoming round); to stand firm, to be established, Psa 93:2 101:7 Job 21:8. Hence נְכֹון הַיֹּום Pro 4:18, fixed, steady day, noon, when the sun seems to stand without moving, at the highest point of its course in the sky; Gr. σταθερὸν ἠ̓͂μαρ, σταθερὰ, μεσημβρία Arab. قايمة النهار; see Schult. on Pro. loc. cit.; Ruhnken ad Tim. p. 236. Figuratively-(a) to be right, fit, Exo 8:22 Job 42:8 Psa 5:10.-(b) to be true, sincere; Psa 78:37, לִבָּם לֹא־נָכֹון עִמֹּו “their heart was not sincere towards him.” Part. f. נְכֹונָה that which is sincere, sincerity, Psa 5:10.-(c) to be firm, constant; רוּחַ נָכֹון a spirit constant in the purpose of virtue, Psa 51:12 Gen 41:32, נָכֹון הַדָּבָר מֵעִם הָאֱלֹהִים “the thing is certainly decreed of God.”-(d) to be firm, intrepid, used of the mind, Psa 57:8 108:2 112:7 .-(e) to be sure, certain, אֶל־נָכֹון certainly, 1Sa 26:4 23:23 .

(2) to be founded, Jud. 16:26 .

(3) to be prepared; Exo 19:11, הֱיוּ נְכֹונִים “be ye ready;” verse 15 Exo 19:15 34:2 Eze 38:7. Followed by לְ of pers. to be ready for any one, i.e. to be near at hand, Pro 19:29 compare Job 15:23 followed by לְ of the thing, to be ready for any thing, i.e. to be near doing it; Psa 38:18, אֲנִי לְצֶלַע נָכֹון “I am near falling.”

Hithpael הִתְכֹּונֵן, once Pro 24:3 elsewhere הִכֹּונֵן-

(1) to be established, confirmed. Pro 24:3 Num 21:27 Isa 54:14.

(2) to prepare oneself. Psa 59:5.

Derived nouns, כֵּן, אָכֵן, כַּוָּן, כִּיּוּן, מָכֹון, מְכֹונָה, תְּכוּנָה and the pr.n. יָכִין, יְכָנְיָה, כָּנְיָה, יְהֹויָכִין.

כון

1Ch 18:8 [Chun], pr.n. of a town in Phœnicia, called in the parallel place, 2Sa 8:8, בֵּרֹתַי. In the itinerary of Antoninus, it is called Conna, see Michaëlis in Suppl. p. 1233.
